next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 15

The International Conference Center smelled of floor polish, new carpet, and overheated coffee from the press mezzanine, the kind of institutional cleanliness that tried too hard to feel calm. Anya cleared the east-side screening with Damian's credential clipped high on her lapel and a reporter's notebook tucked under one arm. The screener barely looked at her. Across the plaza the diplomatic queue crawled between extra guards and handheld scanners, three lines deep already. Whatever Damian had rerouted her around, it had more teeth than inconvenience.

Her seat in the East Gallery put the whole assembly hall under her.

The Main Assembly Hall curved out below in tiers of dark wood and pale stone. Ostanian delegates sat to the west in sober rows. Westalian delegates mirrored them from the east. A raised platform split the room down the middle, with translation booths above and the south wall all glass beyond it, a clean sheet of window looking out to the terrace and the clipped hedges beyond. If somebody got a line through that glass, the podium would sit in it like a target at a fairground.

Four hundred minds hit her at once.

She braced one hand on the armrest and let the noise come through.

*if this runs past four my daughter will kill me...*

*translator three is missing paragraph six again, useless...*

*coffee's burned, of course it's burned...*

*south service stairs at thirteen fifty, don't look at the doors again...*

Anya opened her eyes.

That could have been security. It could have been catering. It could have been somebody arranging a smoke brea...
open_hooks_to_carry: Anya disclosing mission-critical information to Loid — enough to activate him
Yor brings the Garden-conspiracy intelligence from Ch 10 — the family reckoning: the Garden sent her to kill people whose deaths served the conspiracy
Yor's choice: she refuses to operate as a Garden asset; she will fight for her family, on her own terms — a quiet, decisive turn
The family planning session: Loid's tactical chess vs. Yor's direct-action methodology, mediated by Anya's secret telepathic optimization
Anya optimizes the plan using family thoughts they didn't volunteer — the efficiency works, and the fact that it works makes her feel worse
Bond's precognitive episode — barking at a specific location on Loid's map, connecting to Ch 6 vision
Damian arriving at the Forger apartment — the reader knows he knows who Anya is, giving every interaction new weight
Loid's involuntary shift into father-evaluating-romantic-interest mode, readable by Anya

payoffs_to_land.description: Bond's precognitive warning from Ch 6 is reinforced and partially clarified — he barks at a specific location on the summit security map
Yor's doubt from Ch 6, confirmed in Ch 10, reaches its turn — she chooses against the Garden

prev_tail_excerpt:
...re warnings fully landed. She answered questions he had not quite asked. She knew where the pressure was before anyone in the room admitted there was pressure at all. At the state dinner he had called it intuition. At the safe house he had called it field instinct. At the gala he had stopped trying to name it because every name he had sounded insufficient.

That still was not proof of anything except that she remained a problem he could not diagram.

He set the photo down long enough to close the bedroom window. Rain tapped once against the glass and slid away. The room cooled immediately anyway. When he came back to the bed, the photo waited where he had left it, pink hair bright even in bad grayscale.

He had been trained from childhood to treat attachment as exposure. Standing in the kitchen with the basement key in his hand, he tried on the neat answer: cut Anya loose, bury whatever was left of the network, and call it prudence. Crane stayed dead in that version too. Mertens stayed exposed. The mole kept breathing.

He dropped the key into the back of the kitchen drawer under a roll of string and three dead batteries. There would be no more fixed rooms. Nothing on paper would sleep behind walls again. If anything passed between them now, it passed hand to hand or not at all.

Then he went back to the bed, slid the class photo under his mother's note, and shut the lid. He would keep working with her. He would not hand her the photo. Not yet.

He put the box back on the shelf behind the unread histories. Rain tapped once against the shut window. The apartment stayed cold.
secondary_character_beats: Yor's choice should be quiet and matter-of-fact — she is not dramatic about her own pain, but something has broken
Bond's precognitive barking should connect to the earlier dark vision from Ch 6
state_deltas_required: the operation is in crisis; the Forger family must assemble; Yor must confront the Garden's corruption and make a choice about who she fights for

The Forger family enters the operation as a unit. Yor chooses to fight as Yor Forger, not as Thorn Princess — her relationship with the Garden breaks. The plan takes shape. Damian is inside the family circle carrying knowledge the reader can see.
